name: Scenario & Location Comparison Matrix
description: Score and rank multiple scenarios, locations, or strategic options against weighted criteria — with knock-out constraints, an auditable weighted-scoring table, and a sensitivity check on the ranking.
author: accilium

variables.name: OPTIONS
variables.description: The scenarios, locations, or strategic options being compared, with whatever descriptive data is available for each.
variables.example: Site A: Graz, industrial zone, 40min from HQ, land cost 1.2m; Site B: Linz, near rail hub, land cost 1.8m; Site C: Wels, greenfield, land cost 0.9m...

variables.name: CRITERIA
variables.description: The evaluation criteria, with weights if already defined.
variables.example: Logistics access (30%), total cost (25%), permitting risk (20%), workforce availability (15%), expansion room (10%)

variables.name: CONSTRAINTS
variables.description: Hard requirements that disqualify an option regardless of score.
variables.example: Must have rail or highway access within 10km; must allow heavy-industry zoning

example_input: OPTIONS: three candidate sites; CRITERIA: logistics 30%, cost 25%, permitting 20%, workforce 15%, expansion 10%; CONSTRAINTS: rail access required
